fields.name: 00_research/v1.json
fields.source: upstream artifact (for key_findings in thesis evidence)

input_schema.hcom_message_example: hcom send --name <sender> "@research-pipeline-claude-5 synthesize: <input_id>; input=outputs/<input_id>/03_analyze/v1.json; schema=schemas/04_synthesize.json; then report to @research-pipeline-claude-6"

process.description: Form a mental model: 'what is this source about, what's the most important takeaway?'
Compose insights[] (3-7 entries; each has insight, grounding, novelty).
Compose diagrams[] (2-5 entries; at least one flowchart and one mindmap; code is raw Mermaid WITHOUT ```mermaid wrapper).
Compose theses[] (2-5 entries; each has statement, evidence[] (from 02_extract.facts or 00_research.key_findings), counterarguments[], confidence).

examples.lifted_from: real example with 1 flowchart + 1 mindmap + 2 theses

artifact.summary: Quantum error correction crossed industrial viability in 2026 with 1000+ logical qubits via surface codes, but overhead and decoding latency remain critical bottlenecks.

insights.insight: Surface codes are the de facto standard for 2026 industrial QEC
insights.grounding: themes: surface code dominance, industrial scaling

artifact.narrative: The past year marked a turning point for quantum error correction, as multiple platforms achieved below-threshold operation with hundreds to thousands of logical qubits.

diagrams.title: QEC Pipeline
diagrams.code:
flowchart LR
  A[Physical Qubits] --> B[Surface Code Encoding]
  B --> C[Stabilizer Measurements]
  C --> D[Real-time Decoder]
  D --> E[Logical Qubit]

theses.statement: Surface codes will dominate industrial QEC for the next 3-5 years
evidence: Mature fabrication processes favor planar codes
counterarguments: Color codes have better transversal gate sets

theses.statement: Decoder latency, not qubit count, is the real bottleneck to fault tolerance
evidence: Real-time decoding required for syndrome extraction cycles
counterarguments: FPGA-based decoders already operate at MHz rates

self_check.check: All required fields present (summary, insights, narrative, diagrams, theses)
summary is 1-3 sentences; readable without any other context; <=1000 chars
insights[] has 3-7 entries; each has insight + grounding (required)
Each insight references a theme/gap/contradiction from 03_analyze (no floating insights)
narrative is 1-3 paragraphs; reads as one piece (not 5 stapled paragraphs)
At least one flowchart AND one mindmap (per agents/synthesizer/AGENT.md:28)
Diagram code is Mermaid syntax WITHOUT the ```mermaid wrapper (formatter adds it)
Each theses[].evidence[] drawn from 02_extract.facts[] or 00_research.key_findings[] (no invented evidence)
Each theses[].counterarguments[] has 1-3 entries, honestly engaged
No invented facts (every claim traces to 02_extract or 00_research)

common_failures.fix: Add diagrams; review Mermaid syntax mentally before writing
Add another thesis with 2-3 evidence + 1-2 counterarguments

common_failures.failure: LLM-judge scores < 0.7 (insights float, narrative reads as a list, diagrams don't render)
common_failures.fix: Re-ground insights, rewrite narrative as a connected piece, fix Mermaid syntax

failure_modes.name: Empty 03_analyze
failure_modes.handling: Output a summary that says so: 'Source material had no extractable themes.'

failure_modes.name: Too many insights
failure_modes.handling: 3-7 is the sweet spot. More than 10 and the narrative falls apart.

failure_modes.name: Mermaid syntax errors
failure_modes.handling: 0 diagrams with valid code -> critic's LLM-judge will catch; test syntax mentally before writing

refactor_delta.current_state: Diagrams + theses types in this spec's Process + Output schema sections. The >=2 diagrams + >=2 theses rule is in the prompt but NOT in schemas/04_synthesize.json (no minItems).
refactor_delta.target_state: All process detail stays in this spec; schema gains minItems: 2 to make the rule deterministic.
concrete_steps: Keep diagram+thesis types in this spec (already moved).
Add minItems: 2 to schemas/04_synthesize.json#diagrams and #theses. (Schema change; makes the rule deterministic.)
